completion_summary: Implemented and integrated a candidate-only public JSONL subprocess boundary with explicit task/info allowlists, recursive hidden/debug/private-task-text/traceback/absolute-path rejection, finite message and response limits enforced during stdout reads, request-typed student response schemas, generic non-reflecting student errors, and a public agent RNG seed decoupled from hidden world/evaluation seeds. Evidence: all six serious tasks passed real reset/act/update/history message audits; hidden/text/path/traceback negative probes failed closed; an actual separate student subprocess passed reset/act; 27 public-harness/local-eval/public-leakage tests passed; Ruff, core/script mypy, teacher mypy, and claim check passed. Reference evidence: none required for the message-boundary contract. Known limitations: trusted local subprocess is not an OS sandbox and cannot safely run unknown third-party code without an external no-network/read-only/low-privilege resource-limited container; exploit matrix, formal private evaluation, benchmark, and publication claims remain closed. Implementation commit a70bb40; integration commit 126813c.
scope: Enforce and audit a candidate-only independent JSONL public interaction boundary: allowlisted reset/act/update messages, recursive hidden/debug/private-text/traceback/path leakage rejection, bounded and request-typed student responses, real subprocess probing, and explicit trusted-local-not-sandbox semantics across all six serious tasks
